Identification.LongTitle: An Act to repeal the Clarity Act

Provision.Text: This enactment repeals the Clarity Act.
Whereas the QuÃ©bÃ©cois form a nation;
Whereas that nation has been formally recognized by the House of Commons;
Whereas the decision on its future within Canada lies with the QuÃ©bÃ©cois nation, not the federal government;

And whereas the QuÃ©bÃ©cois nation has laws that give its government both the right to consult the people of QuÃ©bec by means of a referendum on the subjects of its choice and the right to determine the wording of the referendum question;
Now, therefore, His Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:

Section.Text: The Clarity Act, chapter 26 of the Statutes of Canada, 2000, is repealed.
